scope.summary: Seed both repositories' cass-memory context with the quota-death, lying-gate, publish-before-persist, and self-fulfilling-fixture anti-patterns and prove they are retrievable.
out_of_scope: Backfilling feedback votes or promoting the new candidate rules to a higher maturity state.
Copying session-history snippets or any field-test/customer identifiers into committed evidence.

entry_points.name: oraclemcp cm context
entry_points.trace: cm context 'swarm quota gates publish persist self-fulfilling fixtures' --json returned success=true with b-omcp-e6-fixture-oracle, b-omcp-e6-quota-death, b-omcp-e6-publish-persist, and b-omcp-e6-gates-lie.

entry_points.name: rust-oracledb cm context
entry_points.trace: The same command from rust-oracledb returned success=true with the four corresponding b-rodb-e6 anti-pattern IDs.

entry_points.name: cross-repo checksum handoff
entry_points.trace: tests/artifacts/evidence/oraclemcp-eng-program-bp8ia.6.6-driver-handoff.json binds server commit e1616e14d89726003d4a1e5d9c923ea179136093 and driver commit 88a1c5f44154c59242ce8986b5ce2183aeb31311 to exact trees, paths, and SHA-256 digests; all values were independently recomputed.

limitations: The new rules are candidate/draft memories and do not yet carry helpful or harmful feedback events.
This close has no required-proof/v1 artifact and remains not-ready on scoped retrieval evidence.
